    "expirience -> experience (Lexis; A2; Low)",
    "habbits -> habits (Lexis; A2; Low)",
    "what take realy a lot time -> that take really a lot of time (Grammar; A2; Medium)",
    "Delite -> Delete (Lexis; A2; Low)",
    "live more free -> live more freely (Grammar; A2; Low)",
    "I beleave my tips help you -> I believe my tips will help you (Grammar; A2; Low)",
    "unlacky -> unlucky (Lexis; A2; Low)"
  ],
  "errors_by_category": {

      {"excerpt": "two my own tips", "correction": "two of my own tips", "comment": "Missing preposition 'of' after numeral; A2 pattern", "impact": "Medium", "cefr_threshold": "A2"},
      {"excerpt": "what help me", "correction": "which help me", "comment": "Wrong relative pronoun 'what' instead of 'which/that'", "impact": "Low", "cefr_threshold": "B1"},

      {"excerpt": "what you study all time", "correction": "that you study all the time", "comment": "Wrong relative pronoun; missing article 'the'", "impact": "Medium", "cefr_threshold": "A2"},
      {"excerpt": "hasn't free time", "correction": "don't have free time", "comment": "Wrong auxiliary 'has' with 'you'; A2-level agreement error", "impact": "High", "cefr_threshold": "A2"},

    ],

      {"excerpt": "give somr motivation to you", "correction": "give you some motivation", "comment": "Indirect object should come before direct object", "impact": "Medium", "cefr_threshold": "A2"}

  ],
    "feedback": "CEFR Level: A2. The text communicates two clear tips with basic discourse organization (Firstly, Secondly). However, there is a high density of spelling errors (abot, reccommend, simplie, somr, futcher, expirience, habbits, realy, Delite, beleave, unlacky) and systematic grammar issues: verb patterns (say→tell, need to count, try noting), relative pronouns (what→which/that), articles, and prepositions. These patterns align with A2-level control. Strength: use of 'networking' shows B1 vocabulary ambition. Grammar score 0.55 reflects acceptable but weak control—meaning is clear, but errors are frequent and sometimes impact comprehension (e.g., 'hasn't free time').",
  "next_steps": [
    "Focus on high-frequency verb patterns: tell someone about something, need to + verb, try + -ing.",
    "Practice the difference between relative pronouns 'which/that' (for things) and 'what' (meaning 'the thing that').",
    "Review spelling of common words: recommend, experience, habits, really, believe, unlucky."
  ]
